name: Black Bean and Cashew Chili
recipeIngredient: 1 can (15 oz) black beans (or Prepare from dried)
1/2 can beer (something with flavor, not Coors Light or a clear dry beer)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large pot, sauté the diced onions and minced garlic in olive oil over medium-low heat until the onions become translucent.
2. Add the seeded jalapeno and Anaheim chiles to the pot and continue sautéing for a few more minutes.
3. Stir in the black beans with their cooking liquid, chopped tomatoes, cashews, spices (cumin, chili powder, cinnamon, cayenne, red pepper flakes), and beer. Bring the mixture to a low boil.
4. Reduce heat to maintain a gentle simmer, stirring frequently. Continue cooking until most of the liquid has evaporated, about 30-40 minutes.
5. About 10 minutes before the desired consistency is reached, add the frozen corn to the chili.
6. Serve hot with rice and warm tortillas. Optionally, serve with a side of nonfat yogurt.
